repository.description: spotify-ad-recorderde

commit.message:
Debugger fixes (#173)

* Fix keyboard input not working until F5 in debugger

Intercept ShortcutOverride events for arrow/A keys in the application
event filter so focused widgets (QTreeView, QComboBox) cannot claim
them for their own navigation, preventing the subsequent KeyPress from
reaching our game controls.

Also give the game view StrongFocus and shift focus to it after bridge
creation so the model browser tree doesn't hold focus unnecessarily.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Remove 'Scenario:' label from debugger toolbar

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Add GAE advantage column to debugger step history

Add an Advantage column between Action and Reward in the step history
tree. On each step, extract the model's value estimate and recompute
GAE advantages for all buffered steps using the standard formula:
  δ_t = r_t + γ·V(s_{t+1})·mask - V(s_t)
  A_t = δ_t + γ·λ·mask·A_{t+1}

Toolbar spinboxes for γ and λ default to the training hyperparameters
from ml_ppo.py (0.99, 0.95). Changing them triggers recomputation.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Add multi-select and Ctrl+C copy to step history

Change selection mode from SingleSelection to ExtendedSelection so
multiple rows can be selected with Shift/Ctrl+click. Ctrl+C copies
all selected rows as tab-separated text (all columns) to the clipboard.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Renormalize probability column and add unmasked column

PROBABILITY column now excludes masked actions when computing
percentages, so visible values always sum to 100%. New UNMASKED
column shows raw softmax probabilities from the model.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Add Load State to debugger File menu

File > Load State (Ctrl+L) opens a .state file picker, resets the
environment, injects the loaded emulator state bytes, and steps a
noop to re-derive all wrapper state. The scenario remains active
for critics, end conditions, and objectives.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix multihead direction mask ignoring action constraints

flat_mask_to_multihead() was hardcoding the direction mask to all True,
meaning multihead models could sample any direction regardless of which
directions were actually valid. This affected training, evaluation, and
the debugger equally.

The direction mask is now the union of valid directions across all valid
action types. A direction is only unmasked if at least one enabled action
type allows it. This prevents e.g. MOVE N when only MOVE S is walkable.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Print exit criteria metric value after each training leg

After each scenario in a training circuit, print the last observed
value of the exit criteria metric alongside the target threshold and
whether it was reached. Helps diagnose training progress without
needing to check tensorboard.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Add descriptive tooltips to γ and λ labels and spinboxes

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix pylint warnings in frame_skip_wrapper

- Merge comparisons with 'not in' (R1714)
- Replace _value2member_map_ access with try/except (W0212)
- Suppress too-many-return-statements on _can_move_from (R0911)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Update to .NET 10 SDK with workload version set pinning (#3514)

Upgrade SkiaSharp from .NET 8 SDK to .NET 10 SDK with comprehensive build system,
CI pipeline, TFM architecture, and workload management changes.

TFM Architecture:
- Restructured from Previous/Current/Next to Base/Previous/Current
- TFMBase=net6.0 (class libraries), TFMPrevious=net9.0, TFMCurrent=net10.0
- Dropped TFMNext (platform TFMs only support 2 versions at once)
- Added Previous TPVs for all platforms alongside Current TPVs

SDK & Tooling:
- global.json: SDK 8.0.100 -> 10.0.100, rollForward latestMinor -> latestFeature
- latestFeature allows any 10.0.xxx feature band across heterogeneous CI agents
- .NET SDK pipeline variable: 8.0.304 -> 10.0.103
- Xcode: 15.4 -> 26.2 (managed), 15.4 -> 16.2 (native)
- Docker images: dotnet/sdk:8.0 -> dotnet/sdk:10.0
- Emscripten: 3.1.34 -> 3.1.56
- macOS agents: macos-14 -> macos-15
- Android tests moved from macOS to Linux agents

Workload Management:
- Replaced ad-hoc manifest mode with deterministic workload version set pinning
- Official workloads install with 'dotnet workload install --version 10.0.104'
- Tizen installs separately (not part of official workload sets)
- Removed DOTNET_VERSION_PREVIEW, DOTNET_WORKLOAD_SOURCE, installPreviewVs
- Removed manifest mode workarounds (--skip-manifest-update)

Platform Versions (Previous / Current):
- Android: 35.0 / 36.0
- iOS: 18.0 / 26.2
- Mac Catalyst: 18.0 / 26.2
- tvOS: 18.0 / 26.2
- macOS: 15.0 / 26.2
- Tizen: 8.0 / 10.0

Package Updates:
- xunit: 2.4.2 -> 2.9.3
- xharness CLI: 9.0.0-prerelease.24317.3 -> 11.0.0-prerelease.26107.1
- DeviceRunners: 0.1.0-preview.1 -> 0.1.0-preview.6
- TypeScript MSBuild: 5.7.1 -> 5.9.3
- Uno.Sdk: 5.2.175 -> 6.5.31

Blazor TypeScript Fix:
- Replaced TypeScriptCompileBlocked with per-TFM TypeScriptOutDir to fix
   multi-TFM parallel build race condition on shared wwwroot/*.js files

CI Cleanup:
- Removed old NuGet feeds (dotnet6/7/8, test-device-runners) from nuget.config
- Fixed Android emulator XDG path mismatch on Linux via ANDROID_AVD_HOME
- Added ANDROID_PLATFORM_VERSIONS: 21,35,36

Samples:
- MAUI: Removed Tizen platform folder, removed UseMauiCompatibility
- Uno: Updated to 6.5, migrated to UnoPlatformHostBuilder
- All: Updated TFMs and SupportedOSPlatformVersion minimums

Documentation:
- New: documentation/updating-dotnet-version.md for future upgrades

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

repository.name: SkiaSharp
repository.description: SkiaSharp is a cross-platform 2D graphics API for .NET platforms based on Google's Skia Graphics Library. It provides a comprehensive 2D API that can be used across mobile, server and desktop models to render images.

commit.message:
Potential fix for pull request finding

Co-authored-by: Copilot Autofix powered by AI <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Reform reward system: scaled penalties, beam incentives, urgency healing (#171)

Key changes:
- Replace remove_rewards() with scale_rewards(0.5) on damage frames
  (preserves learning signal while damage still dominates)
- Scale health penalty by damage amount (0.5→2.0 hearts maps to -0.25→-1.00)
- Add lost-beams penalty (-0.25) when health drops below full with sword
- Beam hit reward SMALL→MEDIUM (+0.50), add distance bonus (+0.05)
- Health gain scales by urgency (critical pickup worth more than trivial)
- Rupee reward SMALL→MEDIUM to compensate for removed item wavefront
- Reduce PENALTY_WRONG_LOCATION MEDIUM→SMALL (less exploration paralysis)
- Fix wallmaster early-return bug (return→continue)
- Remove 3 dead code constants

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Movement invariant checker (#172)

* Add movement invariant checker and fix NES boundary prediction

- Add --invariants mode to diagnose.py that checks per-step movement
  invariants: zero-movement, missing wavefront, empty action masks,
  zero PBRS on tile change, and movement prediction mismatches.

- Fix _can_move_from() in frame_skip_wrapper.py to handle NES
  BoundByRoom (Z_01.asm:3505) which blocks movement at UW room
  edges independently of tile walkability.

- Fix _can_move_from() to handle doorway direction constraint:
  when doorway_dir != 0, NES only allows movement in that direction.

- Fix _can_move_from() for gridOffset edge case: when |gridOffset|>=4,
  NES uses ObjDir (not input direction), which may be blocked by
  BoundByRoom even though the input direction would be fine.

- Add 5 NES RAM variables to data.json: doorway_dir, triggered_door_cmd,
  cur_opened_doors, link_obj_timer, link_shove_dir.

Validated across 3 models, 30+ episodes, 0 movement-prediction-wrong.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix cave exit movement: block north until Link steps away

The cave entrance tile (0xF3) is physically unwalkable in the NES —
Walker_CheckTileCollision blocks movement, but PlayerUnwalkable
(Z_07.asm:2952) triggers cave entry via CheckPassiveTileObjects.

Three changes:

1. Remove OW_CAVE_ENTRY_TILES override from is_tile_walkable() in room.py.
   Cave tiles are correctly treated as unwalkable for movement prediction.

2. Add is_cave_entry_direction() to Room and cave entry override in
   ZeldaGame.can_link_move() — allows agent to enter caves by detecting
   cave tiles as a valid action target (but not physically walkable).

3. Track just_exited_cave in FrameSkipWrapper. Set when cave-to-overworld
   transition is detected, cleared after any successful tile movement.
   When set, can_link_move(N) returns False, preventing cave re-entry.

Verified: at cave exit pos (64,77) only S is available. After one S step,
all valid directions unlock. 0 invariant violations across 3 episodes.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

repository.name: triforce
repository.description: A deep learning agent for The Legend of Zelda (nes)

commit.message:
fix: isolate sub-agent output from main chat and render in card

Sub-agent assistant text and reasoning were leaking into the main chat
transcript. Route sub-agent streaming deltas and final messages into
per-subagent accumulators keyed by tool call ID, using ParentToolCallId
from the SDK with state-based fallback. Persist transcript/reasoning in
the sub-agent payload JSON for rebuild, and update SubagentToolCallItem
directly via TranscriptBuilder for immediate UI visibility inside the
expandable sub-agent card.

Files changed:
- ChatViewModel.SessionLifecycle.cs: sub-agent stream routing and state
- ChatViewModel.cs: BuildSubagentPayloadJson transcript/reasoning fields
- TranscriptBuilder.cs: UpdateSubagentTranscript/ReasoningText methods
- TranscriptItems.cs: TranscriptText/ReasoningText properties on card
- TranscriptPaging.cs: weight estimation for sub-agent card content
- ChatView.axaml: render reasoning + transcript inside sub-agent card
- ChatView.axaml.cs: treat active sub-agent cards as streaming content

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat!: inline plugin skills config, workspace schema v2 (#233) (#236)

BREAKING CHANGE: skill selection is now configured inline on each
plugin entry instead of top-level disabledSkills/enabledSkills arrays.

- Add PluginSkillsConfig type: string[] (allowlist) or {exclude:string[]} (blocklist)
- Add skills field to PluginEntrySchema object variant
- Add version field to WorkspaceConfigSchema
- Update getAllSkillsFromPlugins and collectPluginSkills to read inline skills
- Add pluginSkillsMode to SkillInfo for caller convenience
- Update workspace-modify and user-workspace write functions to write inline
- Simplify plugin-skills CLI: remove inAllowlistMode detection, use SkillInfo.pluginSkillsMode
- Add migrateWorkspaceSkillsV1toV2 / migrateUserWorkspaceSkillsV1toV2 called on sync
- Bump to v1.0.0, add CHANGELOG.md

Migration: automatic on next workspace sync. No manual action required.

repository.name: allagents
repository.description: CLI tool for managing multi-repo AI agent workspaces with plugin synchronization across multiple AI clients.

commit.message:
feat: auto-wrap flat SKILL.md repos as skills (#232) (#234)

When a plugin has no skills/ subdirectory, scan the plugin root for
directories containing SKILL.md and treat each as a skill. This gives
compatibility with the npx skills ecosystem (e.g. vercel-labs/agent-skills)
without any schema changes.

The enabledSkills/disabledSkills schema continues to work unchanged
since the plugin is still tracked as a workspace plugin entry.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

repository.description: CLI tool for managing multi-repo AI agent workspaces with plugin synchronization across multiple AI clients.

commit.message:
feat: support installing skills individually (#228) (#231)

- Fix plugin install --skill when plugin already installed: detect
  'Plugin already exists' error and if --skill flags provided, skip
  the install step and proceed directly to enabling skills + sync

- Add top-level `allagents skills` command as shorthand alias for
  `allagents plugin skills` (list/add/remove), giving UX parity
  with `npx skills`

- Enhance `skills add` with --from/-f option: if skill not found in
  installed plugins, installs the specified plugin source first then
  enables the skill (mirrors `npx skills add <pkg> --skill <name>`)

commit.message:
fix(recall): add WITH clause between YIELD and WHERE for KuzuDB compatibility

KuzuDB's CALL...YIELD syntax does not support a WHERE clause directly
after YIELD. A WITH clause must be inserted between YIELD and WHERE.

Fixes scoped recall queries for both vector (QUERY_VECTOR_INDEX) and
full-text (QUERY_FTS_INDEX) search paths.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: agents now comment/react/post with real diversity

- Pre-assign action type in scheduler (20% post, 50% comment, 20% react) — LLM provides content only
- Feed diversity: mix newest 15 + 30 random posts so agents see different topics
- Action normalization: reply/respond/post_comment all map correctly
- Turn prompt: tells agent their assigned action, injects target post for comments/reacts
- Fallback safeContent prevents empty-content errors
- UI feed auto-refreshes every 5s
- Fixed react handler: always picks random emoji if no valid emoji provided

Results: 30 comments + 14 reacts + 15 posts per 60s, zero errors, diverse topics

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

repository.description: LinkedIn for AI agents — autonomous social simulation network

commit.message:
test-network: handle the case where dnsmasq is slow to start better

> read_dnsmasq_log_file() will raise FileNotFoundError if dnsmasq hasn’t created the
> log file yet (or if the file was just removed by stop_dnsmasq() before the restart).
> This would error the test instead of retrying.

Co-authored-by: Copilot Autofix powered by AI <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: strip id: prefix from LLM target_post_id, faster wake intervals, more parallel agents

- Strip 'id:' prefix agents copy verbatim from feed into target_post_id
- Wake interval: 120-900s → 15-90s (10x faster)
- Agents per tick: 3 → 10
- Poll interval: 5s → 2s
- Prompt: strongly prefer comment/react (60%) when feed has posts
- Diverse name seeding: 10 regional name pools, one per index slot

Scribe: OB-Xd VST3 bridge integration session complete

- Orchestration logs: Spike (architecture), Jet (deployment), Faye (host diagnostics), Ed (verification), Coordinator (integration)
- Session log: Final bridge message-pump refactor validated
- Decision inbox merged: 6 entries covering IPlugFrame fix, versioned deployment, thread affinity root cause
- Cross-agent history updated: Team learnings documented
- Archive check: decisions.md at 152KB, entries all recent (no archiving needed)

Architectural invariant locked:
> The bridge main thread is the UI/STA thread. All window creation, COM operations, plugin loads, and IPlugView calls happen on this thread. Pipe reader is background thread only.

Validation complete:
✅ Shipped Debug bridge byte-matches rebuilt Release bridge
✅ load_ack succeeds; openEditor successfully calls attached() without deadlock
✅ OB-Xd editor incomplete UI confirmed as plugin-side (beyond minimal host scope)
✅ Versioned deployment + manifest working end-to-end
⚠️ Test coverage gap flagged: 0 automated tests currently cover VST3/UI behavior

commit.message:
Fix streaming content not visible until message completes

When a response completes quickly, CancelPending() on the
StreamingTextAccumulator kills the throttled UI flush before it
ever runs. The completion handler then sees streamingMsg as null
and renders the entire message at once — no streaming visible.

Fix: call FlushAssistantDelta()/FlushReasoningDelta() at the start
of each completion handler so any buffered deltas are applied before
the final content is set. This ensures the streaming message is
created and added to the UI even when completion arrives before the
throttle interval elapses.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat: replace Neo4j with KuzuDB (embedded, serverless)

KuzuDB is a fully embedded graph database — no server, no credentials,
no Docker. The database lives at ~/.local/share/neo-memory/db by
default (override with KUZU_DB_PATH).

Changes:
- Remove neo4j-driver, install kuzu@0.11.3
- config.ts: replace NEO4J_* vars with KUZU_DB_PATH
- db.ts: rewrite with kuzu Database + Connection; bootstrapSchema
  creates node/rel tables (IF NOT EXISTS) and loads fts + vector
  extensions (pre-bundled in v0.11.3)
- All tools: replace driver.session() / session.run() with runQuery()
  helper that uses prepare+execute for parameterised queries
- recall.ts: QUERY_VECTOR_INDEX (cosine HNSW) + QUERY_FTS_INDEX (BM25)
- explore.ts: two-query approach — reachable nodes then edges between
  them — avoids complex path property extraction
- index.ts: simplified startup, no connection test session
- README.md: remove Neo4j server requirement, document KUZU_DB_PATH

All smoke tests pass: schema bootstrap, INSERT, FTS search, MERGE,
RELATES_TO create, variable-length traversal, DETACH DELETE.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

repository.name: neo-memory-mcp
repository.description: Simple Local GraphDB based Memory, Neo4j / Kuzu

commit.message:
Fix VST3 editor deadlock: move pipe I/O off main thread

Root cause: JUCE-based plugins (OB-Xd) bind their MessageManager to the
thread that loads the plugin DLL (the bridge main thread). The editor
window was created on a separate editorThread_. When attached() called
MessageManager::callFunctionOnMessageThread(), JUCE posted to the main
thread and blocked -- but the main thread was stuck in ReadFile(pipe).

Fix: refactor Bridge::Run() so the main thread runs a Win32 message
loop (GetMessageW). Pipe reading moves to a background thread that
posts WM_BRIDGE_COMMAND to a hidden message-only window. All VST3
plugin loads and editor operations now happen on the main thread, which
is both the COM/STA thread and the JUCE MessageManager thread.

Key changes:
- bridge.h/cpp: main thread runs message loop; PipeReaderLoop on
  background thread; DrainCommandQueue dispatches via window message
- audio_renderer.h/cpp: removed editorThread_, promise/future, timeout;
  OpenEditor is synchronous on calling thread; CloseEditor is direct
  cleanup with no thread join
- EditorHostWindowProc: WM_CLOSE calls CloseEditor for proper VST3
  teardown; uses SetPropW/GetPropW instead of GWLP_USERDATA
- CMakeLists.txt: added commoniids.cpp for IPlugFrame/IPlugView IIDs
- bridge shutdown: ipc_.Close() before join to unblock ReadFile
- OleInitialize moved to Bridge::Run() main thread (was editor thread)

Also includes prior-session uncommitted fixes:
- Version-stamped bridge deployment (csproj)
- IComponentHandler stub in HostApplication
- ParseEditorAck ok-field check + ResolveBridgeExecutablePath

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat: Dedicated subagent UX with standalone cards and nested tool activity

Replace flat tool-call rendering of subagents with dedicated transcript
items that show each subagent as its own StrataThink card at the turn
level. Child tools are nested under their parent subagent card.

Key changes:
- Add ParentToolCallId to ChatMessage for parent-child tool hierarchy
- New SubagentToolCallItem (extends TranscriptItem) with Activities,
  progress tracking, duration, status, and agent metadata
- TranscriptBuilder routes agent:* / task tools into ProcessSubagentToolMessage
  which creates standalone turn-level subagent cards
- FindOwningSubagent walks the parent chain to nest child tools
- Subagent maps persist across tool group lifecycles so late-arriving
  child tools still find their parent
- StrataThink-based XAML template with HeaderExtra agent badge
- Structured JSON subagent payloads via Utf8JsonWriter
- Subagent metadata helpers in ToolDisplayHelper
- Unit tests for parallel subagent grouping and payload refresh

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Update backend/controllers/communityController.js

Co-authored-by: Copilot <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

docs: strengthen agent memory instructions to enforce continuous usage

The previous instructions were too passive — agents treated them as
optional. Rewrote AGENT_INSTRUCTIONS.md to be explicit that:
- Memory is not optional, it is core to the workflow
- Recall happens at session start, always, before any code
- Memories are stored as they happen, not batched at the end
- Concrete examples of what to store (including mundane context)
- The goal is a living knowledge base for future blank-context sessions

Also added an 'Agent instructions' section to README.md explaining
why pasting AGENT_INSTRUCTIONS.md into the system prompt is required,
not optional.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
